# Issue Tracking Standards

## Two-Tier Rule

| Work Type | Track In |
|---|---|
| Bugs, repo-specific tasks | **GitHub Issues** (default) |
| Epics, cross-repo, sprint planning | **Linear** (link back to GH Issues) |

**Default is always GitHub Issues.** Linear is for cross-repo coordination only.

---

## GitHub Issues

### Required Templates in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`

**bug_report.md:**
```yaml
---
name: Bug Report
about: Report a defect
labels: ["bug"]
---
## What happened?
## Expected behaviour
## Steps to reproduce
## Environment
```

**feature_request.md:**
```yaml
---
name: Feature Request
about: Suggest a new feature or improvement
labels: ["enhancement"]
---
## Goal
## Proposed approach
## Definition of Done
```

### GH Issue Rules

- Labels: `bug`, `enhancement`, `documentation`, `security`, `performance`
- Link PRs via `Fixes #123` in commit message
- Close issues via PR merge — never manually
- Branch names: `fix/123/description`

---

## Linear — Broader Initiatives

Use for: epics spanning repos, sprint planning, velocity tracking, roadmap items, collating GH Issues.

Every Linear ticket with code work **must link** to relevant GitHub Issues:
```markdown
## Related GitHub Issues
- hyperi-io/dfe-engine#123 — Schema migration
- hyperi-io/dfe-loader#456 — Parser update
```

---

## Linear CLI

### Install & Configure

```bash
npm install -g @anthropics/linear-cli
# Get token: Linear Settings → API → Personal API Keys (lin_api_XXXX)
linear config set-token lin_api_XXXXXXXX
linear teams list
```

### Common Commands

```bash
linear issues list --limit 20
linear issues list --status in-progress
linear issues view DFE-123
linear issues create -t "Title" -d "Description"
linear issues update DFE-123 -d "Updated description"
```

---

## Ticket Fields

### Mandatory (every ticket)

| Field | Format |
|---|---|
| **Title** | `<type>: <short description>` |
| **Goal** | One sentence |
| **Definition of Done** | 2-5 checkboxes |
| **Priority** | Linear field: 1=Urgent, 2=High, 3=Medium, 4=Low |
| **Estimate** | Linear field, log2: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 hours |

### Optional (include ONLY if valuable)

| Section | When |
|---|---|
| Components | Multiple systems affected |
| Risk & Guardrails | Production/breaking changes |
| Acceptance Criteria | Complex multi-outcome feature |
| Migration Reference | Data/schema/infra migrations |
| Why Now | Non-obvious urgency |

**Rule:** If estimate > 32, break into smaller tickets.

---

## Priority Scale

| Priority | Value | Response Time |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| 🔴 Urgent | 1 | Drop everything |
| 🟠 High | 2 | This sprint |
| 🟡 Medium | 3 | Next 2-4 weeks |
| ⚪ Low | 4 | When capacity allows |

---

## Title Types

| Type | Example |
|------|---------|
| `feat:` | `feat: add S3 retry logic` |
| `fix:` | `fix: resolve auth timeout` |
| `chore:` | `chore: update dependencies` |
| `docs:` | `docs: add API examples` |
| `refactor:` | `refactor: extract auth module` |
| `perf:` | `perf: optimise query caching` |
| `test:` | `test: add integration tests` |

---

## Good vs Bad Tickets

| ❌ Don't | ✅ Do | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Fill sections with "TBD" / "N/A" | Delete empty sections entirely | Noise wastes reader time |
| Omit priority and estimate | Always set Priority (1-4) + Estimate (log2) in Linear fields | Can't plan without them |
| Over-document a 1h task with 200 words | Scale documentation to complexity | Overhead must match task size |
| Put priority/estimate in description text | Use Linear's dedicated fields | Enables filtering/reporting |
| Write `Definition of Done: Code merged` only | Write 2-5 specific checkboxes | "Code merged" is always true — not a criterion |

### ✅ Simple Task
```markdown
Title: fix: typo in README
## Goal
Fix "recieve" → "receive" in installation section.
## Definition of Done
- [ ] Typo corrected
- [ ] PR merged
Priority: 4 | Estimate: 1
```

### ✅ Complex Task
```markdown
Title: chore: migrate Bitnami images to upstream
## Goal
Replace all Bitnami container images before Aug 28 paywall deadline.
## Components
- dfe-discovery, dfe-core, dfe-ui, dfe-apps
## Risk & Guardrails
- Kafka KRaft changes cluster bootstrap
- Rollback: pin to bitnami-legacy/* if blocked
## Definition of Done
- [ ] All repos updated with new image refs
- [ ] CI/CD passes, Kafka operational in KRaft mode
- [ ] No Bitnami refs remain
Priority: 2 | Estimate: 8
```

---

## AI Assistant Rules

1. **DO NOT fill every section.** Only sections that add value.
2. **MANDATORY:** Goal, Definition of Done, Priority (1-4), Estimate (log2).
3. **DELETE empty sections.** Never write "TBD" or "N/A".
4. **Search before creating.** Check `linear issues list` for duplicates.
5. **Scale docs to complexity.** Typo fix ≠ epic.

---

## LLM Ticket Scripts

### Create Ticket
1. Gather: goal, type, priority (1-4), estimate (log2), DoD (2-5 items)
2. Search: `linear issues list --limit 30` for duplicates
3. Ask user: new ticket, add to existing, or sub-issue?
4. Create: `linear issues create -t "<type>: <desc>" -d "<body>" -p <priority>`
5. Report: ticket ID, URL, priority, estimate

### Update Ticket from Work
1. Identify ticket from branch name or user input
2. `git log --oneline -10` + `git diff main --stat`
3. `linear issues view DFE-XXX` for current state
4. Generate progress comment with changes, files, tests, remaining items
5. `linear issues update DFE-XXX -d "<updated>"`

### Retrospective Ticket (after-the-fact)
1. `git log --oneline --since="1 week ago"` + ask user for goal
2. Reconstruct ticket with all DoD checked
3. Search for existing ticket; create new or update existing

### Find Ticket
1. Check `git branch --show-current` for `DFE-XXX`
2. Search `linear issues list` by keywords from branch/commits
3. Present matches or offer to create new

### Consolidate Tickets
1. `linear issues list --limit 50` for candidates
2. Check overlap: titles, components, criteria, assignees
3. Recommend: merge (duplicate), create parent epic (related), link (loosely connected), or keep separate

---

## Branch Naming

```text
<type>/<ticket-id>/<short-description>
```
`feat/DFE-123/add-retry-logic` · `fix/DFE-456/auth-timeout` · `chore/DFE-789/update-deps`

---

## Workflow

| Phase | Actions |
|---|---|
| **Start** | Find/create ticket → set "In Progress" → create branch `<type>/<ticket-id>/<desc>` |
| **During** | Commit with ref: `fix(DFE-123): resolve timeout` · update ticket if scope changes |
| **Complete** | All DoD checked → PR referencing ticket → status "In Review" |
| **After Merge** | Ticket → "Done" (auto or manual) · add retro notes if applicable |
